name: Much-Easier-Than-it-Looks Triple Chocolate Torte
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Raspberry freezer-jam (If you can't find this, use bottled or canned black raspberries, cook together with 1/2 cup sugar)
recipeInstructions:
Frosting
2 cups powdered sugar
4 tablespoons butter
3 tablespoons Dutch processed cocoa powder
1/3 cup boiling water
Keep the chocolate in the freezer until you need it. Let the jam thaw on the counter.
The hardest part of this is cutting and frosting the cake. To cut, measure the frozen cake into three equal, stackable sections. Mark your measurements with toothpicks. Turn the cake on its side and gently cut it into three layers using an electric knife if available. Put the cake back into the freezer while completing the next step.
In a bowl, mix ricotta cheese, sugar, and 1 tsp vanilla until well blended. Mix with a spoon or hand mixer. Flake off chocolate from the freezer into small chips (about 1/4 the size of chocolate chips or smaller) and gently mix them into the ricotta cheese. The chips should be large enough to remain crunchy when chilled.
Heat raspberry jam or other fruity filling on the stove until hot but not scalding. Add rum (Grand Mariner or Brandy is acceptable) to the fruit filling. Remove from heat.
Place the bottom layer of cake on serving plate and cut four pieces of waxed paper, placing each about 1 inch under each edge. Use waxed paper as a drip catch. If experienced, this step can be omitted.
Apply 1/3 cup hot fruity filling to the center of the cake, using drizzles and chunks. Spread half of ricotta filling over fruit mixture. Place second layer on top and smooth edges with spoon. Repeat with third layer, reserving some syrupy fruit and ricotta for later.
In a deep bowl, mix cocoa powder and powdered sugar with pinch of salt. Melt butter and blend into powdered mixture until it forms small balls. Add remaining warm fruit mixture (1/4 cup) and 1/2 tsp vanilla, blending high for 30 seconds. Gradually add boiling water, mixing high until smooth and runny but thicker than maple syrup or gravy. Spread frosting over top of torte and let it run down sides.
Let cool for a few minutes before removing wax paper. Use remaining ricotta mixture to make a decorative dollop in the middle.
Cover and refrigerate for about 1 hour before serving. Cover with a bowl, not Saran wrap (to avoid sticking to frosting).
This recipe requires no baking or cooking. After practice, it can be completed in about 35 minutes. Impress friends with its appearance and taste. Credit given to a make-a-mix cookbook by the same author.
